content:
- completed story-19 task 02: setup-sql now emits explicit Cockroach cursor-capture plus cursor-parameterized changefeed SQL, runner test support consumes the public setup-sql binary instead of the crate, and setup-sql config ownership is split into Cockroach/Postgres-specific modules with shared table-name parsing
- final verification is green: make check passed, make lint passed, make test passed; make test-long was intentionally not run because this task is not a story-end / long-lane task
- marked the task done with <passes>true</passes>, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed all changes in 67d5467, and pushed master to origin
- should do next: stop here

- refreshed the Ralph task summaries, read the choose-task rules, and loaded the previous progress entry
- confirmed the repo stack is a Rust workspace (runner, setup-sql, ingest-contract) plus the vendored Go MOLT verify service; no active failing bug tasks remain, no active priority tags are present, and only story-22a/02 is explicitly blocked by story-22a/01
- selected story-19 task 03 as the most logical next unblocked failing task because story-19 task 02 just landed, the current code already split setup-sql Cockroach/Postgres concerns, and the next coherent step is to finish the one-time setup image by emitting least-privilege PostgreSQL grants before moving on to runner scratch-image and workflow stories
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: work .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/03-task-emit-the-absolute-minimum-postgresql-role-grants-needed-by-the-runner.md with the tdd skill, then validate make check, make test, and make lint

- wrote the selected relative task path to .ralph/current_task.txt
- no tests were run because the choose-task role requires selecting the next task and quitting immediately once the path is written
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: start story-19 task 03 with tdd and keep validation strict with make check, make test, and make lint

- read saved progress, loaded the current task pointer, and read the tdd plus improve-code-boundaries skill instructions
- confirmed the selected task is story-19 task 03 about emitting the minimum PostgreSQL role grants needed by the runner
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: read the task file itself, inspect the improve-code-boundaries skill directory for smell guidance, then decide whether this turn must stop at a plan or can execute

- read the task file and confirmed it is still pre-execution; this turn therefore needs to end with a new plan file rather than code changes
- inspected improve-code-boundaries smell guidance, especially config-not-reduced, mixed-responsibilities, and display-not-strings, to shape the upcoming design notes
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: inspect the setup-sql crate and existing task-story files, then write a concrete plan under the story plans directory ending in NOW EXECUTE before quitting

- inspected setup-sql postgres grant config/rendering plus runner bootstrap/reconcile code to identify the actual permission floor the runner needs
- the existing postgres grant renderer is already wired through the CLI and multi-database JSON shape, but it overgrants by emitting TEMPORARY and broad CREATE-on-database comments/strings directly from one renderer file
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: write the story-19 task-03 plan with exact TDD slices for narrowing grants and cleaning the render/config boundaries, add the plan path to the task markdown, then stop

- created the task-03 execution plan at .ralph/tasks/story-19-source-sql-emitter-image/03-task-emit-the-absolute-minimum-postgresql-role-grants-needed-by-the-runner_plans/2026-04-19-postgres-minimum-grants-plan.md
- added the matching <plan> pointer to the task markdown and set the plan terminator to NOW EXECUTE so the next turn can execute under the written TDD slices
- investigated the actual runner permission floor before planning: helper schema/tables are created by the runner itself, mapped destination tables are written via upsert/delete, and the current setup-sql postgres grant renderer overgrants with TEMPORARY plus string-heavy rendering
- should do next, after quitting immediately due to context limit: execute the new plan with strict red-green TDD, narrow the emitted PostgreSQL grants to the proven minimum contract, run make check/make lint/make test, then only mark the task complete if all required lanes pass

- read saved progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd plus improve-code-boundaries skill headers
- confirmed the task is story-19 task 03 and prior turn already wrote an execution plan that should now drive implementation if the task file still points to it
- should do next: read the task markdown, the linked plan, and the improve-code-boundaries smell guidance before making any code edits

- read the task file, linked execution plan, and the key improve-code-boundaries smell notes for config reduction, mixed responsibilities, and display boundaries
- confirmed the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE and the intended refactor is to split postgres grant planning from rendering while proving the narrower privilege floor through public tests
- should do next: inspect the current setup-sql renderer/tests plus runner privilege-consuming tests, then start the first red-green slice

- tightened the setup-sql contract test to require least-privilege postgres grants and refactored the renderer into a typed per-database grant-plan with Display-backed statements shared by text and json output
- proved the minimum contract against real runner behavior by converting one bootstrap test and one reconcile test to non-owner roles with only CONNECT+CREATE, schema USAGE, and table-level DML grants
- should do next: remove any dead config/render surface exposed by the refactor, then run make check, make lint, and make test and fix any first failures one at a time

- completed story-19 task 03: narrowed emitted postgres runner grants to the proven minimum contract, extracted a typed per-database grant-plan/display boundary, and tightened bootstrap plus reconcile contracts to prove the runner works with non-owner least privilege
- final verification is green on the finished tree: make check passed, make lint passed, make test passed; make test-long was intentionally not run because this task is not a story-end / long-lane task
- should do next: run .ralph/task_switch.sh, commit all tracked changes including .ralph state with the required task-finished message, push, and stop
